welcome.startSub: A short, thoughtful intro and a few personal touches
welcome.skipSub: I'll figure it out as I go

review.title: Review before we create anything
review.subApprove: Take a look at what we found in your file. For now you're just choosing what to bring in — nothing gets created until the final step, after one last confirmation. You can edit, include, or leave things out.
review.subCreate: Take a look at what we found in your file. Edit, include, or leave things out — only what's checked will be saved.
review.txIssues: {{count}} rows with an invalid amount or date won't be imported as transactions
review.truncated: The file was cut to the first {{cap}} rows (out of {{raw}})

toggle.includedAria: Included — tap to leave it out

client.statusUnsure: We weren't sure about the status — worth a quick check

lead.statusUnsure: We weren't sure about the status — worth a quick check

tx.recurringTitle: Recurring expense — created automatically each month
tx.clientOrphanTitle: This client won't be included — the transaction will be created without a link

session.clientOrphan: This client won't be created — the session will be skipped
session.noDate: No date — we'll place it on an estimated date

confirm.skipImportHint: What you've already set up is saved. You can import the file later from Settings.

dirty.text: Leave without saving? Any edits you made here will be lost.

result.fatalBody: Something went wrong before anything was created — nothing was saved. You can try again, and if it keeps happening we're happy to help.
result.partialTitle: Import finished — but some rows didn't make it
result.failedLine: {{count}} rows didn't make it. Here's why:
result.partialHint: Whatever made it in is already saved. You can continue, or go back and fix the rows that failed.

why.schema: A field that doesn't exist here — probably a column mapped to the wrong place.
why.duplicate: You already have an identical item.
why.check: One of the values isn't valid (for example an unfamiliar date or status).
why.foreignKey: This item is linked to something that wasn't created.
why.noConnection: The connection dropped — you'll need to reconnect.
why.generic: Something didn't work on this row — you can skip it and continue, or fix it and try again.

modal.title: Import from a file
modal.subBody: Choose what's in each sheet and match up the columns — then we'll move to a review before anything is saved.
modal.noData: We didn't find any data in the file — make sure it isn't empty and that it's saved as CSV or Excel.
modal.truncated: Some sheets were cut to their first rows to keep things fast. You can import the rest in a separate file later.
modal.ready: Ready for the review before we create anything.
modal.notReady: Choose what's in each sheet and map the name / amount columns.
modal.pickYear: Pick a year for each monthly sheet

sheet.rawToggle: file preview (to check it was read correctly)
sheet.colsIntro: The grey value is a sample from the first row. In the next step you'll see a full review of all the data before anything is saved.
sheet.noCols: We didn't recognize any columns in this sheet. The header row may not have been read correctly — try opening "Show file preview" to check, or change the type to "Ignore".
sheet.unmapped: There are {{count}} columns we'll set aside unless you pick a field for them — it's fine to ignore them.
sheet.matrixIntro: Mark each row as income or expense. "Skip" leaves a row out (a totals row, for example).
sheet.noYield: Nothing will be created from this sheet yet — make sure the type is right and that the {{field}} column is mapped.
sheet.summaryEmpty: Nothing to import recognized yet. Choose what's in each sheet, and map the name / amount columns.
sheet.summaryLead: In total we'll create (after you confirm in the review):

recognize.title: What did we find in your file?
recognize.sub: Take a moment to confirm we read it right — you can fix anything here.
recognize.summaryEmpty: No data to import yet — check the table types below.

nudge.empty: We didn't find anything to import in this table — pick another type, or leave it as “Don't import”.

csvMap.title: From your file — confirm the column mapping
csvMap.someUnmapped: {{count}} columns weren't recognized — pick a field for them or skip them.
csvMap.allMapped: You can fix anything manually if something's off.
